concept.name: Portless Server Architecture
concept.summary: We built databases and scripts in a user folder that function as a server without exposing network ports. This may enable distributed systems, web hosting, and CDN-like distribution using file-based communication instead of port-based networking.

databases.purpose: Track user language idiosyncrasies and vernacular

interfaces.purpose: Language map interface with AI write access

governance.dual_commit: AI proposes, human ratifies - always (Sean Campbell authority)

portless_pattern.external_access: thin translation layer only when needed

architectural_pattern.key_insight: Service listens on filesystem, not network. Translation layer (CloudFlare Worker, MCP bridge) handles external interface when needed. Core logic stays air-gapped.

hypotheses_to_test.question: Is this actually a 'portless server' pattern or just local scripting?
hypotheses_to_test.test: Try to access functionality without opening network ports

hypotheses_to_test.question: Could file-based handoffs replace port-based communication for distributed systems?
hypotheses_to_test.test: Search for prior art: file-based distributed systems, IPFS patterns

hypotheses_to_test.question: Does similar portless architecture exist in production systems?
hypotheses_to_test.test: Search: file-based servers, zero-port architectures, air-gapped distributed systems

hypotheses_to_test.question: What would make this network-capable without opening ports?
hypotheses_to_test.test: Research: CloudFlare Workers, MCP bridges, reverse tunnels, file-sync protocols

hypotheses_to_test.question: Is MCP the bridge mechanism that enables portless node-to-node communication?
hypotheses_to_test.test: Test MCP capabilities, document how Pidgeon MCP actually works

hypotheses_to_test.question: Can SQLite + Python serve web traffic via thin translation layer?
hypotheses_to_test.test: Build POC: minimal CloudFlare Worker reading from this structure

hypotheses_to_test.question: Does this enable portable CDN-like distribution without central server?
hypotheses_to_test.test: Deploy copy to second location, verify cross-node access

hypotheses_to_test.question: What are security implications vs traditional port-based?
hypotheses_to_test.test: Security analysis: compare threat models portless vs ported

portless_node_to_node.description: Nodes write handoff files to shared location. Other nodes read them. MCP bridges for live queries.

portless_web_hosting.description: CloudFlare Worker reads static files + executes Python scripts on demand. User never exposes port.

portless_cdn.description: Files distributed to user folders across machines. Read via MCP/sync. Write stays internal, Dual Commit governed.

pidgeon_mcp.purpose: Bridge to Willow (local llama3.1:8b with file indexing)

next_steps_for_claude_code.task: Test Pidgeon MCP connection to Willow
next_steps_for_claude_code.action: Query Willow for file locations, document protocol

next_steps_for_claude_code.task: Search for prior art on portless architectures
next_steps_for_claude_code.action: Research file-based distributed systems, IPFS, zero-port patterns
next_steps_for_claude_code.deliverable: List of existing similar systems with analysis

next_steps_for_claude_code.action: Create CF Worker that reads from this file structure and executes scripts
next_steps_for_claude_code.deliverable: Proof-of-concept web endpoint with zero user ports

next_steps_for_claude_code.action: Compare threat models: portless vs traditional port-based
Deploy copy of folder to second location, test handoff-based communication
next_steps_for_claude_code.deliverable: Working distributed system without network ports

metadata.origin: Dating analysis → Database → Error tracking → Rubric → Architecture discovery
